builtForLocalSchools: Stop the leaks · Built for DRC schools

academic.title: Academic
academic.tagline: Report card season shouldn't shut down the office
academic.summary: When enrollments, attendance, and grades live in separate places, report cards take days and errors multiply. One academic cycle—from admission to Programme National report cards—without the end-of-term panic.
academic.desc: Students, classes, timetable, attendance, grades, report cards

highlights.0: Student profiles with guardians, medical notes, and enrollment history
highlights.1: Classes, sections, and streams aligned to DRC grade levels
highlights.2: Timetable builder with teacher and room assignments
highlights.3: Daily attendance with bulk marking and absence alerts
highlights.4: Gradebooks, exams, and report cards mapped to national curriculum strands
highlights.5: Online admissions linked to your public school website

whoItsFor.0: School administrators tired of reconciling records at term end
whoItsFor.1: Teachers losing evenings to attendance lists and grade re-entry
whoItsFor.2: Parents who only see progress when there's already a problem

academic.localContext: Every term without a single academic system, DRC schools re-type the same Programme National data. ShuleOS tracks learning areas and term reports the way you actually work—not the way a foreign tool expects.

finance.title: Finance
finance.tagline: Stop chasing the same balances every month
finance.summary: Unpaid balances hide in notebooks until parents dispute them at the gate. Bursars chase the same names every month—that's not a collections problem, it's a visibility problem. Invoices, mobile money, and WhatsApp reminders tied to every student account.

highlights.0: Flexible fee structures by class, term, or boarding status
highlights.1: Automated invoices with balances and payment history per student
highlights.2: Mobile-money-ready payment links with exact amounts pre-filled
highlights.3: Fee reminders via WhatsApp before due dates
highlights.4: Expense tracking and financial reports for bursars
highlights.5: Staff payroll records alongside school fee collections

whoItsFor.0: Bursars tired of end-of-term reconciliation surprises
whoItsFor.1: Directors who can't trust cash position until someone adds it up
whoItsFor.2: Parents who pay late because balances weren't clear

finance.localContext: Parents expect mobile money and clear fee statements. Every franc unaccounted for in CDF or USD is a dispute waiting at the gate. ShuleOS makes balances visible before collection day—not after.

operations.title: Operations
operations.tagline: Stop losing track outside the classroom
operations.summary: Transport lists on paper, library books that never return, event registrations in WhatsApp threads—these small leaks add up every week. Library, transport, events, and staff in one place before something slips through.

highlights.0: Library catalog with lending, returns, and overdue tracking
highlights.1: Transport routes, bus assignments, and student pickup lists
highlights.2: School events with registrations and campus visit scheduling
highlights.3: Staff directory with roles, departments, and HR records
highlights.4: Inventory-friendly workflows for uniforms and materials
highlights.5: Operational reports for administrators and directors

whoItsFor.0: Operations managers who can't find the latest transport list
whoItsFor.1: Librarians losing books to manual lending logs
whoItsFor.2: Administrators juggling staff records across files

operations.localContext: From school transport lists to prize-giving day registrations, DRC schools lose hours every week on logistics that should take minutes.

analytics.title: Analytics
analytics.tagline: Stop deciding blind at term end
analytics.summary: When attendance, fees, and performance live in separate notebooks, directors spend days preparing reports they should read in minutes. Dashboards for trends, collection rates, and branch comparisons—before problems become crises.
analytics.desc: Dashboards, branch comparison, attendance & finance reports

highlights.0: Executive dashboards for directors and school owners
highlights.1: Attendance heatmaps and chronic absenteeism alerts
highlights.2: Fee collection rates and outstanding balance summaries
highlights.3: Academic performance trends by class, subject, or term
highlights.4: Branch comparison for schools with multiple campuses
highlights.5: Exportable reports for board meetings and ministry submissions

whoItsFor.0: Directors who find out about problems too late
whoItsFor.1: Administrators spending days on reports spreadsheets could generate
whoItsFor.2: Finance teams missing collection targets until term end

analytics.localContext: Term reports and fee collection targets shouldn't take a week to compile. ShuleOS dashboards show what leadership needs in minutes—not days of manual aggregation.

schoolWebsites.title: School Websites
schoolWebsites.tagline: Stop losing families before they walk in
schoolWebsites.summary: Parents discover schools on WhatsApp and Google first. Without a professional site and online admissions, enquiries go to the school that answered fastest—not necessarily yours.

highlights.0: Branded homepage with your logo, colours, and cover image
highlights.1: About, programs, gallery, and contact sections
highlights.2: Online admissions form feeding straight into your admin queue
highlights.3: Public events page for open days and school activities
highlights.4: Three templates: Modern, Classic, and Minimal
highlights.5: Staff login link connecting visitors to the school portal

whoItsFor.0: Schools losing intake to competitors with better online presence
whoItsFor.1: Admissions teams drowning in paper applications
whoItsFor.2: Directors who know first impressions happen on a phone screen

schoolWebsites.localContext: Every intake season without online admissions, families choose the school that made enrolling easy. Capture enquiries before they go elsewhere.

messaging.title: Messaging
messaging.tagline: Stop "come to the office" as your default reply
messaging.summary: Parents don't trust "we'll call you back." Unlogged WhatsApp threads mean disputes with no record. Structured messaging between teachers, parents, and admins—with history tied to every student.

highlights.0: Direct conversations between teachers and guardians
highlights.2: Message history tied to student and staff profiles
highlights.3: Notifications for new messages and urgent alerts
highlights.4: Role-based access so students, parents, and staff see the right threads
highlights.5: Integrates with fee reminders and event updates

whoItsFor.0: Teachers repeating the same update to twenty parents
whoItsFor.1: Administrators with no audit trail for what was communicated
whoItsFor.2: Parents who only hear about problems after they've escalated

messaging.localContext: Congolese parents live on WhatsApp—but unstructured chats lose messages and create disputes. ShuleOS keeps school communication logged and reachable.

parentStudentPortals.title: Parent & Student Portals
parentStudentPortals.summary: Every parent visit for a balance check or grade update costs staff time and erodes trust. Portals put fees, grades, timetables, and assignments on their phone—mobile money payment included.

highlights.0: Parent dashboard with linked children, fees, and attendance at a glance
highlights.1: Pay school fees via mobile money or payment links without visiting the office
highlights.2: View report cards, timetables, assignments, and school events in real time
highlights.3: Student portal for homework submission, grades, library, and messages
highlights.4: Secure login with role-based access - parents only see their own children
highlights.5: Works on mobile browsers and low-bandwidth connections common in the DRC

whoItsFor.0: Parents tired of visiting the office for information they should see at home
whoItsFor.1: Students missing deadlines because assignments weren't visible
whoItsFor.2: Schools whose front office becomes a help desk every fee season

parentStudentPortals.localContext: Most Congolese parents manage school life from their phone. Without a portal, they find out late—and you pay the cost in trust, time, and delayed payments.
